reason: Operator-run backup script installs system PostgreSQL client tooling (postgresql-client) on a one-shot basis. Intended for ad-hoc use on a freshly provisioned host; tracked-version pinning here would block the operator from running it on hosts with newer Ubuntu archives.
Developer bootstrap installs `npm-check-updates` globally (no project lockfile applies). Documented in scripts/README.md as a one-time setup tool.
Developer bootstrap installs `concurrently` globally for the orchestrator scripts. One-time setup; no project lockfile.
Developer bootstrap installs `cross-env` globally. One-time setup; no project lockfile.
WSL/Linux developer-machine bootstrap. Installs base toolchain (curl, ca-certificates, gnupg). Pinning would block fresh OS installs on newer distros.
Developer-machine bootstrap on Linux. System packages installed once for setup; not part of CI supply chain.
Developer-machine bootstrap on Linux. System packages installed once for setup; not part of CI supply chain.
Developer-machine bootstrap on Linux. System packages installed once for setup; not part of CI supply chain.
CodeQL CLI bootstrap on a self-hosted scanner. System tooling (jq) needed for SARIF post-processing; pinned upstream by Ubuntu LTS package set.

CodeQL CLI requires running `npm install` (NOT `npm ci`) to build a native dependency tree it can scan; the lockfile-restoring `npm ci` would skip the postinstall scripts CodeQL needs to observe. Source-of-truth lockfile is committed.
Same as line 92 — CodeQL JS analyzer requires `npm install` to observe build steps.
